title: Step 4: Click  to confirm that you want to delete this  Key or  to leave the key in the project.

sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To perform any of the following actions, you must have
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.

sections.heading: View Programmatic Access to a Project
sections.body:
You can view the details of all service accounts or API (Application Programming Interface) keys that have access
to your project.

To view the details of a service account in a project using the
Atlas UI:

All the service accounts with access to your project are listed.

Click the name of a service account to view its details, including:

- The obfuscated client secret for the service account

- The date the client secret was last used

- The date the client secret was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) addresses from which the service account can access the API (Application Programming Interface)

- The [project role](project-roles) the service account has been assigned

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [list the names
and details of the service accounts that have access to your
project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-/listprojectserviceaccounts).

To list all API keys for your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects apiKeys list [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects apiKeys list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-apiKeys-list).

You can view the API (Application Programming Interface) access list entries for a project
API (Application Programming Interface) key using an `atlas organizations` command.

To list IP access list entries for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list <apiKeyID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-list).

To view the details of an API (Application Programming Interface) key in a project using the
Atlas UI:

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click View Details.

The \<Public Key> API Key Details modal displays the following information:

- The obfuscated Private Key

- The date the key was last used

- The date the key was created

- The IP (Internet Protocol) address or addresses from which the API (Application Programming Interface) key can
  access the API (Application Programming Interface)

sections.heading: Change Programmatic Access Roles for a Project
Step 3: Click the name of a service account.
Step 4: Edit the .
sections.body:
- Click **Edit Permissions**.

- From the **Project Permissions** menu, select the
  [project role](project-roles) for the
  service account.

- Click **Save and next**.

> **Important:** The service account credentials remain active until they expire
> or are revoked.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [update
service account details for a project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateprojectserviceaccount).

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Edit Permissions.

sections.heading: Step 3: Click the name of a service account.
Step 4: Edit the .
sections.body:
- To add an IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this service account, click
  **Add Access List Entry** and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address.

  You can also click **Use Current IP Address** if the host
  you are using to access Atlas also will make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
  using this service account.

- To remove an IP (Internet Protocol) address from the access list, click
  trash-alt to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address.

- Click **Save**.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [update
service account details for a project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateprojectserviceaccount).

You can edit the API (Application Programming Interface) access list entries for a project API (Application Programming Interface)
key using `atlas organizations` commands.

sections.heading: Add an API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entry for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
sections.body:
To create an IP access list entry for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists create [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-create).

sections.heading: Delete an API (Application Programming Interface) Access List Entry for the API (Application Programming Interface) Key
sections.body:
To delete an IP access list entry for your API key using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete <entry> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas organizations apiKeys accessLists delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-organizations-apiKeys-accessLists-delete).

- Click  to the right of the API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Edit Permissions.

> **Note:** Selecting Edit Permissions takes you to the
> organization level of the Atlas console.

You cannot modify an existing API (Application Programming Interface) Key access list entry.
You must delete and re-create it.

- Click  to the right of the IP (Internet Protocol) address to
  remove it.

- Add the new IP (Internet Protocol) address or CIDR (Classless Inter-Domain Routing) block from which you want
  Atlas to accept API (Application Programming Interface) requests for this API (Application Programming Interface) Key. Use one of
  the two options:

  - Click Add access list Entry and type an IP (Internet Protocol) address, or

  - Click Use Current IP Address if the host
    you are using to access Atlas will also make API (Application Programming Interface) requests
    using this API (Application Programming Interface) Key.

- Click Save.

sections.heading: Revoke Programmatic Access to a Project
Step 3: Remove the service account.
sections.body:
- Click the Trash icon under **Actions**
  to the right of the service account you want to remove from
  the project.

- Click **Remove from this project**.

> **Important:** The service account still exists in the organization and any existing
> credentials remain active until expired or manually revoked.

You can use the Atlas Administration API to [remove
a service account from a project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deleteprojectserviceaccount).

To delete an API key for your project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects apiKeys delete <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects apiKeys delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-apiKeys-delete).

To delete an API (Application Programming Interface) key from a project using the Atlas UI:
